# Doc-drift patterns — strings that MUST NOT appear in any tracked file
# outside `docs/adr/` (where they are allowed for historical context).
#
# Add an entry whenever you delete or rename a file, symbol, or image tag
# that the rest of the repo might still reference. CI enforces this on
# every push.
#
# One literal pattern per line. Lines starting with `#` are comments.
# Patterns are passed to `grep -F` (fixed-string), not regex.
#
# Maintenance: when an entry has been clean for ≥ 1 month after the last
# stale reference was removed, it's safe to delete the entry from this
# file too — the goal is to catch fresh drift, not maintain a memorial.

# 2026-05-19 — single-image refactor (ADR 0008 + ADR 0009)
Dockerfile.gpu
GPU_CONTAINER.md
build_gpu_image.sh
push_gpu_image.sh
build_image.sh
push_image.sh

# Image tag rename. Patterns are bounded by `:` and `/` to avoid
# false positives on substrings like `sdfb-gpu-something`.
sdfb-gpu:
sdfb-gpu/

# 2026-05-21 — vllm_spike.py deleted (no local GPU; §11 Dataflow probe is the
# vLLM validation path). Catch any re-introduced reference.
vllm_spike

# 2026-05-21 — GCS model-layout convention moved from `{project}-models` to
# `{bucket}/synthetic/models/...` (matches the deploy workflows). The old
# token survives only in docs/adr/ (excluded from this scan) as superseded
# history; it must not reappear in live code/config/docs.
project}-models
project>-models
